reconfigure.title: Poise — reconfigure room
reconfigure.description: Pick the room sensor and the thermostat/TRV to control. Optional inputs improve accuracy.

data.name: Room name
data.window_sensor: Window / contact sensor (optional — pauses heating when open)
data.climate_mode: Climate mode (auto / heat only / cool only)
data.cool_min_outdoor: Cooling outdoor lockout — cool only above this °C (set low for internal-gain rooms)
data.heat_max_outdoor: Heating outdoor lockout — heat only below this °C
data.comfort_weight: Comfort vs efficiency (0 = save energy, 100 = comfort)

data_description.temp_sensor: Use a free-standing room sensor away from the radiator — NOT the thermostat's/TRV's built-in sensor, or the room model learns wrong.

room.title: Poise — add a room
room.description: Pick the room sensor and the thermostat/TRV to control. Optional inputs improve accuracy.

data.name: Room name
data.window_sensor: Window / contact sensor (optional — pauses heating when open)
data.climate_mode: Climate mode (auto / heat only / cool only)
data.cool_min_outdoor: Cooling outdoor lockout — cool only above this °C (set low for internal-gain rooms)
data.heat_max_outdoor: Heating outdoor lockout — heat only below this °C
data.comfort_weight: Comfort vs efficiency (0 = save energy, 100 = comfort)
data.controls_boiler: This room may request the shared boiler
data.declared_power: Declared heating power (for load shedding / weighted demand, optional)
data.design_flow_temp: Design flow temperature for this zone (optional)
data.source_policy: Heat-source policy (set by your energy automation, optional)

data_description.temp_sensor: Use a free-standing room sensor away from the radiator — NOT the thermostat's/TRV's built-in sensor, or the room model learns wrong.

system.title: Poise System
system.description: Shared-resource coordination across zones. Leave both boiler actions empty to stay diagnostic-only (shadow); set them to let Poise switch the boiler. Enable 'may request the boiler' on each participating room.

data.boiler_count_threshold: Heating zones required to request the boiler
data.max_power_sensor: Maximum allowed power sensor (load shedding, optional)
data.current_power_sensor: Current total power sensor (load shedding, optional)

init.title: Fine-tuning
init.description: Adjust tuning without a reload — the learned model is kept. All fields have sensible defaults.

data_description.comfort_base: The centre of the comfort band; the category sets how wide the band is around it.
data_description.comfort_weight: 0 = save energy (wider band), 100 = tightest comfort. Default 70.

data_description.comfort_start: Set both start and end to enable a daily comfort window; leave both empty for all-day comfort.
data_description.setback_delta: How far to relax the band outside the comfort window.

data_description.adaptive_cool: Let the cooling edge follow the outdoor running mean (EN 16798-1), capped by the ASR ceiling in Advanced.
data_description.cool_min_outdoor: Only cool when it is warmer than this outside; set low for rooms with high internal gains.
data_description.heat_max_outdoor: Emergency brake — only heat below this outdoor temperature. Saving is done by the schedule and presence, not this.

data_description.presence_home: One or more person/device_tracker/group entities. When none is home the zone eases to away setback and stops preheating. Empty = off.
data_description.occupancy_sensor: One or more motion/occupancy sensors. An empty room relaxes to Eco after the delay below. Empty = off.
data_description.absence_after_min: How long the room must read empty before easing off (default 30).

data_description.cool_hard_cap_c: ASR A3.5 ceiling for the adaptive cooling edge (default 26 °C). Lowering it cools more; raising above 26 is an opt-in.
data_description.thermal_shock_delta_k: Largest indoor↔outdoor gap on hot days; the cooling setpoint is raised to stay within it. 0 = off.
data_description.actuator_dynamics: How fast the device reacts. Automatic detects it from the device type.
data_description.compressor_guard: Hold cool/dry starts and flips that would short-cycle the compressor.
data_description.compressor_min_off_s: Wait after a stop before a restart (blank = 300).
data_description.trace_recording: One JSONL line per tick for offline replay analysis (advanced).

data_description.annual_heating_kwh: For the savings estimate only. ~12000 for a house, 6000–8000 for a flat.
data_description.price_eur_kwh: For the savings estimate only. ~0.11 €/kWh for gas, ~0.30 for electricity.

options.auto: Automatic (on if the device can cool)

sensor_unavailable.title: Poise: room sensor unavailable
sensor_unavailable.description: The room temperature sensor `{entity}` is unavailable, so Poise cannot control this room. Check the sensor or device, then this issue clears automatically.

actuator_unavailable.title: Poise: thermostat unavailable
actuator_unavailable.description: The controlled thermostat/TRV `{entity}` is unavailable. Poise cannot write a setpoint until it returns; this issue clears automatically once it is back.

sensor_frozen.title: Poise: room sensor frozen
sensor_frozen.description: The room temperature sensor `{entity}` has not changed for a long time and may be stuck (dead battery, stalled integration). Poise paused learning for this room; control continues on the last value. The issue clears once the sensor updates again.

mould_protection_inactive.title: Poise: mould protection inactive
mould_protection_inactive.description: The configured humidity sensor `{entity}` is unavailable, so Poise cannot compute the mould-avoidance minimum temperature for this room. Frost protection still applies, but mould protection is off until the sensor returns. Check the humidity sensor; this issue clears automatically.

device_schedule.title: Poise: thermostat's own schedule is active
device_schedule.description: The controlled thermostat `{entity}` has its built-in weekly schedule switched on. It will fight Poise's control. Turn the device schedule off; Poise then owns the setpoint.

device_alarm.title: Poise: thermostat reports a fault
device_alarm.description: The thermostat `{entity}` reports a valve/installation fault (e.g. not mounted correctly, valve failure, wrong calibration, or a broken link to the external sensor). Heating may not work; check the device.

low_battery.title: Poise: thermostat battery low
low_battery.description: The battery of `{entity}` is low. Sensing and valve actuation may degrade — replace the battery soon.

operative_unsupported.title: Poise: operative input not available
operative_unsupported.description: Operative TRV input is enabled, but no usable external-temperature input (`{entity}`) is available for this thermostat. Poise fell back to air-side control. Configure a valid external-temperature number, or turn off operative input.

sensor_at_heat_source.title: Poise: room sensor may be at the heat source
sensor_at_heat_source.description: The learned thermal time constant for `{entity}` is implausibly short — typical of a sensor mounted on or near the radiator (e.g. a TRV's built-in sensor). Poise needs a free-standing room sensor away from the heater; otherwise the room model and comfort control degrade.

valve_stuck.title: Poise: valve may be stuck
valve_stuck.description: The radiator valve's calibration step count (`{entity}`) is near zero, which usually means the valve failed to calibrate or is mechanically jammed. The room may not heat correctly. Re-pair or re-calibrate the TRV; the issue clears once a normal step count is reported.

frost_zone_not_boiler.title: Poise: freezing room not controlling the shared boiler
frost_zone_not_boiler.description: These rooms are at frost-protection temperature but are not set to control the shared boiler, so it will not fire to protect them: {zones}. If such a room is heated by the shared boiler, enable "This zone controls the boiler" in its options (a cooling-only room can be ignored). This issue clears automatically once no such room is freezing.
